CROSS-DEFENDANT CITY OF SOUTH SAN FRANCISCO'S ("CITY") MOTION FOR FEES PURSUANT TO CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE SECTION 1038 AGAINST LUEN SHEE SHEK AND SHEKS CONSTRUCTION COMPANY ("SHEK") IS GRANTED IN PART. THE CITY IS ENTITLED TO RECOVER ITS ATTORNEY'S FEES AND OTHER COSTS INCURRED IN DEFENDING SHEK'S CROSS-COMPLAINT FROM AUGUST 24, 2018 ON. THIS MOTION IS CONTINUED TO MAY 19, 2021. SHEK MAY RESPOND TO THAT FILING BY MAY 12, 2021. THE COURT ALSO ENCOURAGES THE PARTIES TO MEET AND CONFER REGARDING AN APPROPRIATE FEE/COSTS AWARD PRIOR TO THE NEW HEARING DATE. ORDER SIGNED. Cross-Defendant City Of South San Francisco?S Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Declaring Lack Of Good Faith And Reasonable Cause In Bringing Proceeding Under Tort Claims Under Ccp 1038 And An Award Of Post Ccp 998 Costs
Off Calendar May-19-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances (via ZOOM videoconference):
Atty. Rhonda Woo, Esq. of Howard Rome Martin & Ridley LLP, 650.980.1039,for the Cross-Defendant City of South San Francisco;
Atty. Gary Selvin, Esq. of Selvin Wraith Helman LLP, 510.874.1811, for Defendants/Cross-Defendants/Cross-Complainants Luen Shee Shek and Sheks Construction Co. Inc.

CROSS-DEFENDANT CITY OF SOUTH SAN FRANCISCO'S Motion For Order Declaring Lack Of Good Faith And Reasonable Cause In Bringing Proceeding Under Tort Claims Under CCP 1038 And An Award Of Post CCP 998 Costs.

The matter is called. ARGUMENT is held. At the conclusion of argument, the tentative ruling is adopted as follows:

The Court found in its April 27, 2021 order that Cross-Defendant City of South San Francisco is entitled to recover its attorney's fees and other costs incurred in defending Defendants and Cross-Complainants Luen Shee Shek and Shek Construction's cross-complaint from August 24, 2018 to the present. Under Code of Civil Procedure section 1038, the City is entitled to "all reasonable and necessary defense costs, in addition to those costs normally awarded to the prevailing party." Section 1038 defines defense costs to include "reasonable attorney's fees," as well as expert witness fees and other expenses "where reasonably and necessarily incurred in defending the proceeding." (Code Civ. Proc. sec. 1038(b); see also, e.g., Austin B. v. Escondido Union School District (2007) 149 Cal.App.4th 860, 888.) Where, as here, the moving party provides the court with "detailed time records and a declaration from counsel establishing the defense costs incurred," plus "a breakdown of fees incurred commencing from various points of time," such a showing is sufficient to support a motion for defense costs under section 1038. (Suarez v. City of Corona (2004) 229 Cal.App.4th 325, 338.).

The City shows that it incurred $195,930.00 in attorneys' fees and $38,864.53 in costs in defending the litigation for the period in question, for a total of $234,794.53. Shek does not challenge the reasonableness of the City's counsel's hourly fees (which range from $120 per hour for paralegal services to $200-210 for attorney time), nor the reasonableness of the costs incurred by the City. However, it argues that the City's counsel spent an excessive amount of time litigating the case, and offers a number of examples of time entries that it contends illustrate its point, including lengthy blocks of time spent summarizing depositions and preparing client reports. The Court finds some modest merit to Shek's challenges. Accordingly, the Court will apply a 10% percent discount to the fees sought, or $19,593.00, which reduces the total fee award to $176,337.00. Based on its independent review of the City's invoices and the accompanying breakdowns, and its experience with the litigation, the Court finds that the City necessarily incurred the remaining fees sought, and that the amount sought, as adjusted, is reasonable in amount.

(See Word minutes for the Court's complete ruling.)

Order signed.

Notice And Motion By Cross-Defendant City Of South San Francisco For Change Of Venue; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; Declaration Of Todd H. Master; Request For Judicial Notice; Proposed Order
Off Calendar Aug-20-2018 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Anna Dubrovsky, Esq. of Anna Dubrovsky Law Group, 415.746.1477, appearing for Plaintiff Ramon Gomez;
Rhonda Wu, Esq. of Howard Rice, 650.980.1839, appearing for Cross-Defendant City Of South San Francisco;
Robin D. Korte, Esq. of Selvin Wraith Halman LLP, 415.404.8720, appearing by CourtCall for Defendants/Cross-Defendants/Cross-Complainants Luen Shee Shek and Sheks Construction Company;
Vincent Tong, Esq. of Tong Law, 510.371.7835, appearing by CourtCall for Defendant Zers Development Inc.

Ruling: ARGUED. At the conclusion of argument, the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows with one modification: Cross-defendant City of South San Francisco's motion to change venue to San Mateo County is denied and its alternate motion to sever the Shek cross-complaint and transfer that cross-complaint to San Mateo County is also denied. Per still valid and binding case law, a cross-complaint for indemnity and contribution is not an action for "injury" based on "negligence" within the meaning of CCP 394. (Tutor-Saliba-Perini Joint Venture v. Sup. Crt. (1991) 233 Cal.App.3d 736, 743-744; City of Chico v. Sup. Crt. (1979) 89 Cal.App.3d 187, 191.) While this court may disagree with the reasoning underlying this rule of law, this court is required to follow and apply holdings of all published court of appeal decisions where, as here, those holdings have neither been overruled nor disagreed with. The City of South San Francisco fails to show that venue should be transferred per CCP 395. The City does not present evidence defeating all other bases for venue under that statute. The City does not present sufficient evidence showing that venue should be transferred based on the convenience of witnesses and ends of justice. (CCP 397.) The City merely relies on the pleadings and a declaration from counsel, which is insufficient. The request to sever the cross-complaint and transfer that portion of the action to San Mateo County is contrary to the interests of judicial economy. Order signed as modified in open court to include the language that the motion is denied without prejudice. (see order for full text)

Judge: Harold E. Kahn, Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR 8600, 415.551.5868, Email: torreanom@aol.com/mtorreano@sftc.org. =(302/HEK)
